index.heading: Binary Search Helper
index.description: Find the safe range of numbers you can guess while still guaranteeing you'll find the answer within your remaining guesses.

errors.lowGtHigh: Low must be less than or equal to High

algorithm.title: How the Safe Range Works
algorithm.description: Understanding the algorithm behind the number guesser

problem.title: The Problem
problem.text: A number is hidden in the range [lo, hi]. Each turn, you pick a number and an oracle tells you "higher", "lower", or "correct". You have G guesses remaining. Which numbers are safe to guess — meaning you can still guarantee finding the answer no matter what the oracle responds?

insight.title: Key Insight
insight.text: With G guesses, binary search can handle at most {formula} numbers in the worst case. When you guess number x, you split the range into:
insight.leftPart: Left part: [lo, x-1] of size (x - lo)
insight.rightPart: Right part: [x+1, hi] of size (hi - x)
insight.conclusion: For your guess to be "safe", both parts must be solvable with only G-1 guesses remaining — regardless of which side the oracle points to.

formula.title: The Formula
formula.intro: A guess at position x is safe if and only if:
formula.solving: Solving for x, where N = hi - lo + 1:
formula.impossibleNote: If Safe Low > Safe High, no safe guess exists — it's impossible to guarantee finding the number.

proof.title: Why It Works (Proof Sketch)
proof.text1: A binary search tree of depth D has at most {formula} internal nodes. Each internal node represents one guess, and each leaf represents "number found". After guessing x, the worst case is whichever side is larger. If both sides have size ≤ {constraint}, then G-1 guesses suffice regardless of the oracle's response.
proof.text2: The safe range is exactly the set of positions where this constraint holds for both sides simultaneously — giving you freedom to pick any number within it.

vizNumberLine.title: Visualization: Number Line
vizNumberLine.subtitle: Showing range [{lo}, {hi}] with {guesses} guesses remaining
vizNumberLine.explanation: The highlighted zone contains {count} safe numbers. Guessing too close to either edge risks creating a subproblem too large to solve in the remaining guesses.
vizNumberLine.noSafe: No safe range exists — the range is too large for the remaining guesses.

vizTree.title: Visualization: Binary Search Tree
vizTree.subtitle: Each guess splits the remaining range. A tree of depth G covers 2^G - 1 numbers.
vizTree.explanation: A tree of depth G has at most 2^G - 1 internal nodes (guesses) and 2^G leaves (outcomes). Each path from root to leaf represents a worst-case sequence of oracle responses. For a guess to be safe, both subtrees must fit within the remaining depth.
